nativeRange.habitat: Rocky subtidal environments and reef-associated zones within the photic zone

imageCandidates.notes: Isaac Chacon, 1024x768. Tack-sharp, vividly red-pink fleshy toothed blades on a clean single-subject shot showing the diagnostic delicate blade-like thallus; out-of-water live specimen.

Frederic Ducarme, 4000x3000. In-situ underwater shot of the whole reddish-pink fleshy plant in reef habitat; slight underwater blue-green haze and surrounding algae, kept as non-recommended habitat context.

summary: Pink Dragon's Breath (Halymenia floresia) is a decorative red macroalgae prized for its soft reddish-pink, fleshy blade-like fronds — a close relative of the more common Dragon's Breath (H. durvillei). It suits display placement in reef tanks and tends to be very sensitive to shipping stress.
careNotes:
Pink Dragon's Breath (Halymenia floresia) is a red macroalgae in the family Halymeniaceae, order Halymeniales — the same family as its sibling species Dragon's Breath (H. durvillei), Dragon's Tongue (H. dilatata), Leafy Ogo (H. maculata), and Orange Galaxy (H. stipitata). It is distinguished from the more common Dragon's Breath by its softer pink coloration and fleshy, blade-like thallus rather than the intensely branched, flame-like form of H. durvillei. Pink Dragon's Breath is rarely encountered in the aquarium trade and is considered an uncommon specialty specimen. It is described as 'an attractive reddish-pink macroalgae that grows in fleshy, delicate blades.' The species is VERY sensitive to shipping and typically requires overnight delivery for a live arrival guarantee, consistent with other Halymenia species in the hobby.

No species-specific husbandry parameters have been published for H. floresia; the water parameters listed are carried over from well-documented congeners and represent standard reef conditions. Temperature is generally advised around 25.6–28.3°C (78–83°F), pH 7.8–8.4, salinity SG 1.023–1.025, and alkalinity 8–12 dKH. Moderate lighting is generally recommended for Halymenia species to maintain coloration without browning. Herbivorous fish (tangs, rabbitfish) and sea urchins have been known to consume Halymenia species readily; it is generally advised to keep Pink Dragon's Breath away from dedicated herbivores. Propagation is primarily by fragmentation. As a photosynthetic organism it does not require direct feeding, though trace element supplementation may support coloration in some systems. Growth rate tends toward medium based on comparable Halymenia species.

DO NOT BURY IN SAND — as a red alga, this species will MELT at the base if the attachment point is buried in the substrate. Attach to rock using superglue, zip-tie or fishing line, or by wedging into a crevice. (Sand-bed placement is generally suitable only for genera such as Caulerpa, Halimeda, Penicillus, Rhipocephalus, and Udotea, along with some calcified Dasycladales.)

primary.name: Mosaic Macros
primary.notes: Sole hobby trade source found for this species. Confirmed scientific name Halymenia floresia; described as reddish-pink fleshy blades; noted as VERY sensitive to shipping. No specific water parameter data provided.

additional.name: Wikipedia — Halymenia
additional.notes: Confirmed genus taxonomy: family Halymeniaceae, order Halymeniales, class Florideophyceae. H. floresia listed as a representative species of the genus with worldwide warm-temperate and tropical distribution. No aquarium husbandry data.

additional.name: AlgaeBarn — Dragon's Breath (H. durvillei)
additional.notes: Sibling species cross-reference. Water parameters (25.6–28.3°C, pH 7.8–8.4, SG 1.023–1.025, kH 8–12), care level, and lighting recommendation extrapolated from this well-documented congener for H. floresia, which lacks its own hobby parameter data.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros general macroalgae care guide
additional.notes: Slice-wide secondary source — provides authoritative Mosaic-wide parameter targets (temp 22-26°C, pH 8.1-8.4, Ca 400-450 ppm, Mg 1250-1350 ppm, NO3 5-20 ppm, PO4 0.02-0.1 ppm) and the 4-method propagation standard. Added retroactively 2026-06-08.
